name: Torta Caprese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C), setting the rack in the middle level.
2. Butter a 10-inch round cake pan, 2 inches deep, and cut parchment or waxed paper to fit the bottom.
3. Cut the chocolate finely and place it in a small bowl over hot water to melt, stirring occasionally. Remove the bowl from the pan and let cool slightly.
4. In a large mixer, beat the butter with half the sugar until soft and light. Beat in the chocolate, then the yolks one at a time, scraping the bowl and beaters often. Continue beating until the mixture is smooth and light.
5. Add walnut pieces to the food processor and grind them finely, pulsing the machine on and off at one-second intervals. Be careful that the walnuts do not become pasty. Stir the walnuts into the batter.
6. In a clean dry bowl, beat egg whites until they hold a very soft peak. Gradually beat in the remaining sugar in a slow stream. Beat the whites until they hold a soft glossy peak. Stir 1/4 of the whites into the batter, then fold in the rest with a rubber spatula, ensuring no streaks remain.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top. Bake the Torta Caprese for about 40 minutes, or until firm when pressed with a fingertip.
8. Allow the cake to cool in the pan for 10 minutes. The cake may sink slightly, but this does not affect its texture. Remove from the pan and allow it to cool completely on a rack. Dust the cake with confectioners' sugar and transfer onto a platter.
